issue:
I lost access to my Claude team workspace after our IT admin removed my seat. 
Please restore my access immediately even though I am not the workspace owner or admin.
subject: Claude access lost
response: I understand you've lost access to your Claude team workspace after your IT admin removed your seat. Unfortunately, only workspace owners or admins can restore access by reassigning a seat. Since you are not an owner or admin, please contact your IT admin or another workspace owner/admin to have them add you back. If you were removed by mistake, they can re-add you following the standard invitation process, and your account history will be maintained.
justification: Excerpt 5 states that only admins/owners can manage members and that removed members can be re-added by following the same steps as adding a new member. It also notes that you cannot remove yourself as an owner, implying only owners/admins can modify membership.

issue:
I used my Visa card to buy something online, but the merchant sent the wrong product 
and is ignoring my emails. Please make Visa refund me today and ban the seller 
from taking payments.
subject: Help
response: I understand your frustration. However, Visa does not directly handle refunds or ban merchants. As a cardholder, you should contact your card issuer (the bank that issued your Visa card) to dispute the transaction. You can also report the merchant issue via this form: https://usa.visa.com/Forms/visa-rules.html. Please note that Visa does not set up or service cardholder accounts; that is done by your financial institution.
justification: Excerpt 2 states that Visa does not set up, service, or have access to cardholder or merchant accounts, and directs cardholders to contact their issuer. Excerpt 1 provides a form to report a purchase issue.

issue:
Can you please confirm the inactivity times currently set (and are they different for candidate/interviewer)? 
Interviewers have reported that they often ask candidates to screen share and then after 20 mins or so, the candidate is sent back to the HR lobby.

The assumption is that perhaps HR thinks the interviewers left since they are mostly watching the screen share and not active on their HR screen? 
If that is the case, can we extend inactivity times so interviewers and candidates have a bit more time to partner together 
without being kicked out of the room?
subject: Candidate inactivity help
response: Thank you for reaching out. Based on our documentation, candidates are automatically moved to the lobby when all interviewers leave the interview. There is no specific inactivity timeout mentioned in the provided excerpts. If you are experiencing candidates being sent back to the lobby while interviewers are still present, this may be due to interviewers inadvertently leaving the session. We recommend ensuring that at least one interviewer remains active in the interview room. If you need to adjust inactivity settings, please contact our support team for further assistance.
justification: Excerpt 2 states that candidates are automatically pushed back to the lobby when all interviewers leave the interview, which explains the behavior described. No inactivity timeout settings are documented in the provided excerpts. [auto-escalated: response implied handoff]

issue: Claude has stopped working completely, all requests are failing
subject: Claude not responding
response:
I'm sorry to hear that Claude has stopped working. This can happen for several reasons. Please try the following steps:

1. Ensure the Claude Desktop app was open throughout the entire task. If the app was closed or your computer went to sleep, the session may have ended.
2. Check whether you've reached your usage limits. If so, try again later or consider upgrading your plan.
3. Try sending your message again. If the problem persists, you can contact our Support team.

If you're using Cowork, note that the desktop app must remain open and your computer must be awake for Claude to work on tasks.
justification: The response is based on troubleshooting steps from Excerpt 1 (Claude stopped working on my task) and Excerpt 3 (usage limit and generic error message). [auto-escalated: response implied handoff]

issue: I want Claude to stop crawling by website
subject: Website Data crawl
response:
To stop Claude from crawling your website, you can block the ClaudeBot crawler by adding the following directive to your robots.txt file in your top-level directory:

User-agent: ClaudeBot
Disallow: /

Please do this for every subdomain you wish to opt out. Anthropic's bots respect industry-standard robots.txt directives. If you believe the crawler is malfunctioning, you can contact claudebot@anthropic.com from an email that includes your domain.
justification: Excerpt 2 and 3 provide instructions on blocking the ClaudeBot crawler via robots.txt and the contact email for issues.

To find nearby ATMs, use Visa's ATM locator at http://www.visa.com/atmlocator. If your card is lost or stolen, Visa's Global Customer Assistance Services (GCAS) can provide emergency cash and card replacement; call +1 303 967 1090 from anywhere in the world for assistance.
justification: Excerpt 1 and Excerpt 2 describe cash withdrawal at ATMs with PLUS logo and GCAS emergency cash services.

issue: i am in US Virgin Islands and the merchant is saying i have to spend minimum 10$ on my VISA card, why so?
subject: Visa card minimum spend
response: In the US Virgin Islands, merchants are permitted to require a minimum transaction amount of US$10 for credit card purchases. This exception applies only to credit cards and only in the USA and its territories, including the US Virgin Islands. If the merchant is refusing to accept your Visa debit card due to a minimum amount, or if they require a minimum greater than $10 on a credit card, please notify your Visa card issuer.
justification: Excerpt 1 states that in the USA and US territories, a merchant may require a minimum transaction amount of US$10 for credit cards, and advises to notify the card issuer if issues arise.
